item.title: Manager/in Nachhaltigkeit & Umwelt (w/m/d)
item.description: Manager/in Nachhaltigkeit & Umwelt (w/m/d) ZVEI - Zentralverband der Elektro- und Digitalindustrie Frankfurt am Main or Brussels Der ZVEI ist der Verband der Elektro- und Digitalindustrie. Als Teil unseres dynamischen ZVEI-Teams und gemeinsam mit unseren mehr als 1.100 Mitgliedsunternehmen und 170 Kolleginnen und Kollegen gestalten Sie aktiv die Zukunft mit Ihren "Electrifying Ideas" und profitieren dabei von einem breiten Netzwerk in einer der innovativsten Branchen Deutschlands. Nutzen Sie die Möglichkeit, Ihre Expertise in zukunftsweisenden Technologien einzubringen. Bei uns erwartet Sie eine offene Unternehmenskultur mit flachen Hierarchien, die Raum für Ihre persönliche und berufliche Entwicklung bietet. Werden Sie Teil unseres engagierten Teams, das die digitale und nachhaltige Transformation vorantreibt. Wir suchen zum nächstmöglichen Zeitpunkt Verstärkung für unser engagiertes Team im ZVEI e. V. am Standort Frankfurt am Main oder Brüssel Manager/in Nachhaltigkeit & Umwelt (w/m/d). Ihre Aufgaben Vertretung der gemeinsamen Interessen unserer Mitglieder gegenüber politischen, gesellschaftlichen und wirtschaftlichen Entscheidern in Deutschland und Europa mit Fokus auf Nachhaltigkeits- und Umweltthemen; Erarbeitung und Bündelung übergreifender Positionen und Stellungnahmen in enger Abstimmung mit unseren Fachverbänden und Bereichen zu Themengebieten wie Circular Economy, Chemikalienregulierung, Ecodesign und Berichtspflichten; Koordinierung von Schnittstellenthemen, z.B. horizontale Ecodesign-Vorgaben oder Stoffe, die das Recycling erschweren; Monitoring und Analyse technologischer, politischer und regulatorischer Entwicklungen, insb. nationaler und europäischer Gesetzgebungsprozesse und ordnungspolitischer Rahmenbedingungen im Bereich Nachhaltigkeit und Umwelt; Eigenständige Betreuung und Weiterentwicklung von Gremien (inkl. inhaltlicher und organisatorischer Vorbereitung, Begleitung und Nachbereitung) zu oben genannten Themen; Pflege sowie Aus- und Aufbau unseres Netzwerks zu Politik, Institutionen und Verbänden in Deutschland und Europa; Steigerung der Sichtbarkeit des Bereichs Nachhaltigkeit und Umwelt durch gezielte Kommunikation, Veranstaltungsorganisation und Kontaktaufnahme zu politischen Entscheidungsträgern. Ihr Profil Erfolgreich abgeschlossenes Hochschulstudium, vorzugsweise im Bereich Umweltwissenschaften, Nach-haltigkeitsmanagement, Wirtschafts- oder Gesellschaftswissenschaften; Erste Berufserfahrungen in einem Verband, einer politischen Institution oder einem Unternehmen sind wünschenswert; Ausgeprägtes Interesse an umwelt- und nachhaltigkeitspolitischen Fragestellungen sowie den regulatori-schen Rahmenbedingungen auf nationaler und europäischer Ebene insbesondere im Kontext von Circular Economy, Chemikalienregulierung und Ecodesign; Idealerweise Erfahrung in Public Affairs/Lobbying oder der Zusammenarbeit mit politischen Entschei-dungsträgern; Analytische und kommunikative Fähigkeiten sowie die Fähigkeit, komplexe Sachverhalte verständlich aufzubereiten; Hohe Eigenverantwortung, strategisches Denkvermögen, Gestaltungswille sowie Freude an der Arbeit im Team; Engagierte Arbeitsweise, Eigeninitiative, Flexibilität und grundsätzliche Reisebereitschaft; Sehr gute schriftliche Ausdrucksfähigkeit und Sprachkenntnisse in Wort und Schrift neben der deutschen mindestens auch der englischen Sprache. Unser Angebot Zuschuss zur betrieblichen Altersversorgung; 30 Tage Urlaub + zusätzlich betriebsfrei zwischen 24.12 – 31.12; Gleitzeit & Möglichkeit zum mobilen Arbeiten; Vermögenswirksame Leistungen; Betriebliche Unfallversicherung; Je nach Standort unterschiedliche Zuschüsse zur Mobilität; Kantinenzuschuss (Standort Frankfurt) bzw. Pluxee Gutscheine (Standort Berlin/Brüssel); BUK Familienservice; Kostenfreie Getränke (Kaffee & Wasser); ...und einiges mehr. Haben wir Ihr Interesse geweckt? Dann freuen wir uns auf Ihre aussagekräftige Bewerbung inkl. Zeugnissen, Gehaltsvorstellung und frühestmöglichem Eintrittstermin. Mehr Informationen über den ZVEI unter www.zvei.org . Mehr Informationen zu unserer Datenschutzerklärung unter www.zvei.org/datenschutz . HIER BEWERBEN ZVEI e. V. Verband der Elektro- und Digitalindustrie Amelia-Mary-Earhart-Str. 12 60549 Frankfurt am Main Telefon: +49 69 6302-0 Fax: +49 69 6302-269 E-Mail: personal@zvei.org , www.zvei.org Apply Now Don't forget to mention EuroBrussels when applying.

item.title: Sustainability Procurement Advisor
item.description: The European Organisation for Astronomical Research in the Southern Hemisphere (ESO) is the foremost intergovernmental astronomy organisation in Europe and the world's most productive ground-based astronomical observatory. ESO carries out an ambitious programme focused on the design, construction and operation of powerful ground-based observing facilities enabling astronomers to make important scientific discoveries. ESO operates three unique world-class observing sites in northern Chile: La Silla, Paranal and Chajnantor and the ESO Headquarters are located in Garching, near Munich, Germany. At Paranal, ESO operates the Very Large Telescope, the world's most advanced visible-light astronomical observatory, and will host and operate the southern array of the Cherenkov Telescope Array Observatory, the world's largest and most sensitive high energy gamma-ray observatory. ESO is a major partner in ALMA, the largest astronomical project in existence. And on Cerro Armazones, ESO is building the 39-metre Extremely Large Telescope (ELT), which will become "the world's biggest eye on the sky" and whose operations will be fully integrated into the Paranal Observatory. For its Quality and Sustainability Office in Garching, within the Office of the Director General, ESO is opening a Paid Associate position of Sustainability Procurement Advisor. The Sustainability Procurement Advisor will support the implementation of ESO's Sustainable Procurement Principles and Guidelines, which are built on three pillars: social, environmental, and financial sustainability. The role focuses on turning these principles into practical, actionable procurement practices by conducting research, analysing procurement documentation, and developing tools that help ESO integrate sustainability criteria systematically and effectively into tenders. The Advisor will also contribute to monitoring and reporting on implementation progress. The position is ideal for someone who is analytical, structured, and motivated to advance sustainable procurement in a complex scientific and technical environment. Key Responsibilities Research and Benchmarking: Conduct research on sustainability standards, certifications, and benchmarks relevant to ESO procurement (e.g., ISO 14001, ecolabels, social compliance certifications, energy efficiency standards) and propose substantiated ways to integrate them into procurement processes; Investigate approaches to Life Cycle Costing (LCC) in public procurement and assess how LCC can be applied to ESO's procurement categories; Analyse Statements of Work (SoW), Technical Specifications (TS), and evaluation criteria to determine: whether sustainability requirements are already included or missing, whether existing criteria can be reused in future procurements; Assess the quality and relevance of sustainability requirements using the six-step methodology defined in ESO's guidelines. Monitoring and Data Collection: Develop overviews (tables, diagrams, dashboards) of recent or recurring procurements, showing: which sustainability criteria were included, the type of criteria (environmental, social, financial), whether they were pass/fail, scored, or descriptive, key drivers and barriers; Build a structured dataset to support sustainability reporting, KPI development, and future procurement planning; Support the Sustainability and Diversity Officer (SDO) and Requesters in reviewing supplier documentation such as sustainability reports, certifications, and human rights statements. Knowledge Base and Tools Development: Contribute to a living knowledge base containing: category-specific sustainability criteria; templates and standard formulations for SoW, TS, and evaluation criteria; case studies from ESO procurements. Draft user-friendly factsheets summarizing key standards, criteria, and sustainability entry points for each procurement category. Additional Possible Contributions: Support the SDO in preparing training materials or awareness-raising content for internal teams; Assist in lightweight post-tender reviews of selected procurements; Help document progress on sustainable procurement implementation across ESO; Provide support to the SDO in other sustainability-related areas as needed. Essential qualifications and competencies Strong analytical and research skills, with the ability to synthesise complex information; Interest in sustainability standards, responsible supply chains, and public procurement practices; Excellent written communication skills and ability to produce clear, structured documentation; Ability to work independently, manage multiple tasks, and collaborate with diverse stakeholders. Desirable qualifications and competencies Familiarity with procurement processes, sustainability reporting, or Environmental, Social and Governance (ESG) frameworks; Experience with data structuring, dashboards, or basic data analysis tools; Knowledge of Life Cycle Costing methodologies. Qualifications Bachelor's degree in sustainability, procurement, public policy, engineering, economics, or a related field. Language Skills An excellent command of English is essential. A working knowledge of German and/or Spanish would be an advantage. Remuneration and Contract We offer an attractive remuneration package including a competitive salary (free of income tax for applicants from ESO member states), comprehensive pension scheme and medical, educational and other social benefits, as well as financial help in relocating your family and support to place your child/children in daycare. The contract is a Paid Associate for a period of one year. The initial contract is for a fixed term duration of one year with the possibility of one fixed-term extension subject to individual performance and organisational requirements and as defined in the applicable policies and staff rules and regulations. For any further information, please visit https://www.eso.org/public/jobs/conditions/paidassoc/ . Our Salary and career structure: ESO's salary structure is based upon a range of career paths which reflect the nature and level of our roles. Each career path is made up of two or three grades which are used to further reflect experience and performance. The role of Sustainable Procurement Advisor role is in Career Path IV . Please follow this link for more details https://www.eso.org/public/jobs/conditions/intstaff/salary-structure/ . ESO aims to support members of personnel in maintaining a good work-life balance ( https://www.eso.org/public/jobs/conditions/intstaff/#work-life-balance ) between their professional and private life. ESO is also committed to offering family-friendly support ( https://www.eso.org/public/jobs/conditions/intstaff/#family-friendly-support ), creating a work environment and policies which allow staff to balance their professional and private responsibilities through flexible working arrangements and financial support for families. The initial contract is for a fixed-term duration of one year with the possibility of a fixed-term extension subject to individual performance and organisational requirements and as defined in the applicable policies and staff rules and regulations. For any further information, please visit https://www.eso.org/public/jobs/conditions/paidassoc/ . Duty Station Garching near Munich, Germany. The role is based on-site at ESO and relocation to the duty station area is required. Staff at ESO benefit from a mobile working policy that allows for limited hybrid working for work-life balance purposes. Application If you are interested in working in areas of frontline science and technology and in a stimulating international environment, please visit http://www.eso.org for further details. Applicants are invited to apply online. Applications must be completed in English and should include a motivation letter and CV. Within your CV, please provide the names and contact details of three persons familiar with your work and willing to provide a recommendation letter upon request. Referees will not be contacted without your prior consent. Deadline for applications is 15.08.2026. ESO Values An important element in any successful employment relationship is harmony in values between an organisation and its people. The ESO Values are: ESO strives for excellence through innovation. ESO provides outstanding services to its communities. ESO fosters diversity & inclusion. ESO believes in the key role of sustainability for its future. Achieving the above are recognized as only possible on the basis of personal values and attitudes that we expect from our employees: respect, integrity, accountability, commitment, collaboration, and clear & open communication . Applicants to any ESO role are asked to reflect on their affinity with these values and advised they may be asked about them if called for interview. Diversity ESO has established diversity as an important value of the Organisation, is committed to providing an equal opportunities environment and is actively seeking to promote a diverse, equitable and inclusive workforce. Please visit https://www.eso.org/public/about-eso/sustainability/dei-at-eso/ for further details. Nationality No nationality is in principle excluded from employment at ESO, however, recruitment preference will be given to nationals of our Member States, host states and strategic partners: Australia, Austria, Belgium, the Czech Republic, Denmark, Finland, France, Germany, Ireland, Italy, the Netherlands, Poland, Portugal, Spain, Sweden, Switzerland, the United Kingdom and Chile, irrespective of gender, age, disability, sexual orientation, ethnicity or religion. Don't forget to mention EuroBrussels when applying.

item.title: Chief Impact Officer
item.description: Chief Impact Officer EIT Water Aarhus, Denmark About EIT Water EIT Water is Europe's innovation community for water, marine and maritime systems. It connects industry, innovators, researchers, educators and policymakers to tackle the most urgent water challenges from scarcity, drought and flooding to pollution and ecosystem decline. EIT Water is part of the European Institute of Innovation and Technology (EIT), a body of the European Union. Founded in 2026, EIT Water has eight regional hubs across Europe and a mandate to deliver systemic innovation from source to sea. EIT Water is now establishing the governance structures that will guide its first Strategic Agenda and long-term impact. The Role EIT Water is recruiting its inaugural Chief Impact Officer (CIO) in its start-up phase to shape and deliver the overarching impact vision, and ensure that the organisation generates measurable, large-scale societal, environmental, and economic outcomes aligned with its Strategic Agenda and the European Institute of Innovation and Technology (EIT) mission. The CIO holds enterprise-wide accountability for impact strategy, performance, financial sustainability and value creation across the full EIT Water portfolio , integrating impact into all core pillars: education, innovation, business creation, and regional engagement. The CIO is also responsible for ensuring delivery of a high impact Regional Innovation Scheme (RIS) Strategy. As a key strategic support to the CEO, the CIO drives system-level transformation, ensuring the EIT Water delivers both policy-aligned outcomes and long-term sustainable impact at scale. They will work in partnership with the CFOO who will ensure the organisation has the underlying infrastructure in place to deliver on the strategic objectives and impact and performance targets. How to Apply Applications should be submitted via the Apply Now button by 5pm CET Friday 24 th July 2026 and should include a current CV and a cover letter setting out your interest and relevant experience. The appointment process is led by Perrett Laver on behalf of EIT Water. For questions about the process, please contact Jess.winfield@perrettlaver.com . EIT Water is part of the European Institute of Innovation and Technology (EIT), a body of the European Union. This activity is co-funded by the European Union. EIT Water is committed to equal opportunities and welcomes applications without discrimination on any grounds. EIT Water is an established Danish Association based in Aarhus. Apply Now Deadline 24 July Don't forget to mention EuroBrussels when applying.
item.dc:creator: EIT

item.title: Chief Finance and Operations Officer
item.description: Chief Finance and Operations Officer EIT Water Aarhus, Denmark About EIT Water EIT Water is Europe's innovation community for water, marine and maritime systems. It connects industry, innovators, researchers, educators and policymakers to tackle the most urgent water challenges from scarcity, drought and flooding to pollution and ecosystem decline. EIT Water is part of the European Institute of Innovation and Technology (EIT), a body of the European Union. Founded in 2026, EIT Water has eight regional hubs across Europe and a mandate to deliver systemic innovation from source to sea. EIT Water is now establishing the governance structures that will guide its first Strategic Agenda and long-term impact. The Role EIT Water is recruiting its inaugural Chief Finance and Operations Officer (CFOO) to build and lead the organisation’s financial and corporate backbone during its start-up phase and through future growth and scaling. The CFOO reports to the CEO and serves as a member of the Executive Leadership Team, with regular engagement with the Supervisory Board and its relevant committees. The CFOO holds executive responsibility for financial strategy, governance, operations, and organisational enablement, ensuring the organisation operates with strong discipline, compliance, and scalability. Alongside the Chief Executive Officer and Chief Impact Officer and supported by a leadership team of Directors, the CFOO will play a critical role in shaping organisational strategy and enabling sustainable growth, including the design of appropriate legal, financial, and operational structures to support expansion, partnerships, and investment activities. Given EIT Water’s start-up phase, the CFOO will initially oversee a broad corporate services portfolio, with the organisational model expected to evolve as the organisation scales. The mandate includes: Finance & Operations, Legal & Procurement, Data & Technology, and People & Organisation. How to Apply Applications should be submitted via the Apply Now button by 5pm CET Friday 24 th July 2026 and should include a current CV and a cover letter setting out your interest and relevant experience. The appointment process is led by Perrett Laver on behalf of EIT Water. For questions about the process, please contact Boris.Evenhuis@perrettlaver.com . EIT Water is part of the European Institute of Innovation and Technology (EIT), a body of the European Union. This activity is co-funded by the European Union. EIT Water is committed to equal opportunities and welcomes applications without discrimination on any grounds. EIT Water is an established Danish Association based in Aarhus. Apply Now Deadline 24 July Don't forget to mention EuroBrussels when applying.

item.title: Health - Public Affairs - Trainee Consultant
item.description: Health - Public Affairs - Trainee Consultant Acumen Public Affairs Brussels, Belgium Are you passionate about promoting health at both European and Global level? Are you a policy nerd? Are you proactive, solution-driven, willing to take the extra mile and eager to help us grow the business by delivering excellent service to our clients? Do you want to be part of an amazing team? Then this opportunity is perfect for you! What we are looking for: We are looking for a Trainee Consultant to join and strengthen our Healthcare practice. The ideal candidate is proactive, solution-oriented, enjoys taking initiative, eager to go the extra mile and has excellent attention to detail. The role includes daily monitoring of various issues (legislative and non-legislative), conducting research, drafting newsletters and policy briefings, helping in organising trainings and workshops, and providing overall support to more senior staff members. Requirements: Essential: EU national or work permit holder as the traineeship is in Brussels, Belgium; A previous traineeship in the health and/or pharmaceutical sectors. Candidates without experience in these sectors will not be considered; Perfect command of English; additional language skills an asset; Strong planning skills; Advanced research and writing skills, including the ability to summarise issues quickly and accurately; Strong PowerPoint skills, including the ability to translate complex information into clear, visually compelling presentations; Ability to work in a team, whilst at the same time carrying out own tasks independently and to agreed deadlines; Flexibility, enthusiasm and ability to work under tight deadlines in an international environment; Proficient with IT and new media tools. Desirable: Previous experience/traineeship in one of the European Institutions – especially in DG SANTE or the Parliament’s SANT Committee; Good, demonstrated command of social media – experience in organising social media campaigns; Previous experience in organisations working on Global and Public Health (WHO, GAVI, Global Fund, World Bank, OECD etc.). Apply Now Deadline 31 July Don't forget to mention EuroBrussels when applying.
item.dc:creator: Acumen Public Affairs

item.title: Policy and Public Affairs Interns
item.description: Policy and Public Affairs Interns (Two Positions – CIP Contract – 6 Months) AmCham EU - American Chamber of Commerce to the EU Brussels, Belgium € 1700 per month Job description: AmCham EU is seeking to recruit two Policy and Public Affairs interns for the period of September 2026 until the end of February 2027. The successful candidates will support AmCham EU’s Policy and Public Affairs team by contributing to its policy, advocacy, external communications and stakeholder engagement activities. The roles involve conducting research, monitoring EU policy developments, drafting communications materials, preparing briefings and supporting meetings and events with policymakers, member companies and other stakeholders. The positions offer a unique opportunity to gain first-hand experience of EU advocacy across a broad range of European and transatlantic policy issues. Main tasks: Support all stages of advocacy activities, from policy research and development to communications and stakeholder engagement; Monitor and research EU policy developments relevant to AmCham EU member companies; Prepare briefing notes, background papers and other policy and communications materials; Draft a wide range of written materials, including correspondence and meeting reports; Assist with the preparation, coordination and follow-up of meetings with policymakers, business representatives and other stakeholders; Ensure the accuracy and integrity of internal databases; Support the organisation of conferences, events and committee meetings. Qualifications/skills/experience required: Recent graduate with a Master’s degree in European studies, political science, international relations, law, economics or a related field; Strong interest in European affairs and public policy; Strong organisational and project coordination skills, with a proactive mindset, high attention to detail and the ability to manage multiple priorities simultaneously; Excellent written communications skills; Strong interpersonal skills and a collaborative approach to working with colleagues; Computer literacy, particularly Microsoft Office; Familiarity with AI tools and an understanding of their responsible use in a professional environment are an asset. Languages: Fluent English (both written and spoken); other EU languages can be an asset. Contract: Belgian CIP contract (convention d’immersion professionnelle) . Stipend: EU 1700/month. How to apply: Any candidate interested in applying for the position should send a CV and cover letter to policyvacancy@amchameu.eu before Friday, 10 July 2026 . Please note that due to the large volume of applications we receive, AmCham EU will contact only successful candidates. AmCham EU is committed to the principle of equal employment opportunity for all employees and to providing employees with a work environment free of discrimination and harassment. Qualified candidates will receive consideration for this position without regard to race, colour, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender perception or identity or national origin. We are committed to creating a diverse and inclusive workforce that reflects society and a workplace based on principles of equality. Don't forget to mention EuroBrussels when applying.

item.title: Health - Public Affairs - (Senior) Account Manager
item.description: Health - Public Affairs - (Senior) Account Manager Acumen Public Affairs Brussels, Belgium Are you passionate about promoting health at both European and Global level? Are you a policy nerd? Are you proactive, solution-driven, willing to take the extra mile and eager to help us grow the business by delivering excellent service to our clients? Are you also able to lead small teams and guide junior colleagues? Do you want to be part of an amazing team? Then this opportunity is perfect for you! What we are looking for: We are looking for a Public Affairs (Senior) Account Manager who will work with and complement our Healthcare practice based in Brussels, Belgium. The role includes managing a client portfolio, drafting reports and policy briefings, participating in strategy meetings, identifying research needs, supporting new business efforts by contributing to pitches and proposals, providing overall support to more senior staff members and guiding junior members of the team. What you should bring to the table: EU national or Belgian work permit holder; Perfect command of English; additional languages are an asset; A client-oriented mindset, ability to lead projects and teams and deliver high quality outputs; Advanced writing skills and ability to summarise complex issues efficiently; Excellent communication skills; ability to adapt writing to different audiences, and to convey information clearly and visually; Strong organisational and project management skills, balancing multiple deadlines, delivering client projects on time, and keeping everything running like clockwork proven through concrete previous examples and track record; Flexibility, enthusiasm and ability to work under tight deadlines in an international environment; Ability to work in a team, whilst at the same time carrying out own tasks independently and to agreed deadlines; Proficient with IT and new media tools; Good references. Experience and knowledge: 4 - 5 years of relevant work experience for a Public Affairs Account Manager position. The ideal candidate would have a combined experience of working at a health-related role in the European institutions and in an agency, patient or professional group. Candidates without a track record in the healthcare area will not be considered; Demonstrated knowledge of ongoing health debates at EU level (IP, access, vaccines, pharmaceutical strategy, EU’s pharmaceutical legislative framework, rare diseases, etc.); Knowledge of the EU institutions and legislative processes; Experience in policy communications. Apply Now Deadline 31 July Don't forget to mention EuroBrussels when applying.

item.title: Strategic & Digital Communications Intern
item.description: Strategic & Digital Communications Intern EURELECTRIC - Union of the Electricity Industry Brussels, Belgium The Union of the Electricity Industry - Eurelectric is the sector association which represents the common interests of the electricity industry at pan-European level, plus its affiliates and associates on several other continents. We currently have over 34 full members, representing the electricity industry in 32 European countries. As intern, you will: Write weekly in-depth newsletters for our LinkedIn readership; Coordinate the newsletter service dedicated to Eurelectric members; Prepare first drafts for op-eds, news pieces & SEO articles; Proofread policy papers; Support the team with any ad-hoc communication tasks related to campaigns, projects, events, media etc. Requirements and competencies: Currently enrolled in, or recently graduated from, a Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Communications, Journalism, European Studies, Political Science, International Relations, Energy/Climate Studies or another relevant field; Team player with a pro-active/dynamic personality, eager to learn; Proven interest in the essential role that electricity will play for Europe’s decarbonisation, energy security and competitiveness a plus; Full literacy in WordPress, Mailchimp, social media posting a plus; Native English or equivalent. Our offer Eurelectric offers a paid internship. Base allowance as per contract for professional integration (CIP). Only candidates who meet the requirements will be contacted for an interview. The post is available from September 2026 for a period of about 6 months (September 2026 – February 2027). Please submit your application by 24 July, including a CV and motivation letter to grondinelli@eurelectric.org . Attn. Giuseppina Rondinelli, HR Manager, Tel +32(0)471813802 Don't forget to mention EuroBrussels when applying.

item.title: Partnerships and Fundraising Manager
item.description: Partnerships and Fundraising Manager AGE Platform Europe Brussels, Belgium € 2000 - € 2500 per month gross Location: Brussels, Belgium (*), or remote work with periodic visits to headquarters in Brussels Working hours: Part-time (+/-10 days per month) Reporting to: Secretary General Contract type: Employee contract in Belgium, one-year with possibility of extension to unlimited contract; or Self-employed consultant contract, one-year with possibility of extension. Monthly gross salary: Employee contract: range of 2000-2200 EUR depending on candidate’s profile and experience; or Self-employed consultancy: max. €2500 VAT excl. (*) If you are not an EU citizen, please indicate whether you currently hold a work permit for Belgium. Background AGE Platform Europe (AGE) brings together over one hundred organisations of and working for older people, with a mission to enable everyone to live a full and dignified life based on human rights and age equality throughout the life. To that end, we empower older people to get involved in debates on ageing and to impact policy making toward inclusive societies for all ages: “We are the experts of our own lives. Our community is extremely diverse, but our difference is our strength when we speak together. As self-advocates, we have the potential to contribute expertise, skills and experience that enrich our societies and make them more just, inclusive and prosperous.” To know more about our vision, mission, core values and principles, and advocacy goals, see AGE's Strategy 2026-2030 ‘Towards a Society for All Ages’ . Purpose of role AGE is seeking a proactive and self-driven Partnerships and Fundraising Manager to identify and build the right partnerships to raise money which will help AGE to advance its mission. You will expand our reach, enhance fundraising efforts and, eventually, create lasting impact for age equality in old age. We are looking for a person with proven experience and tenacity, recognising that securing funds for the cause of age equality and the human rights of older people requires sustained effort and a creative approach. Whether integrated into the AGE staff in Brussels or as a self-employed independent consultant, you will play a crucial role in diversifying our revenue streams. This effort is part of an initiative to reinforce external fundraising, supplementing contributions from AGE members and institutional grants. With firsthand experience in managing an innovative fundraising environment, your passion and drive to make a difference and achieve results are crucial. These qualities will help progress towards the AGE's vision of a Society for All Ages . Primary responsibilities As Partnerships and Fundraising Manager, you will be expected to uphold the organisation’s vision and core values, as set out in its 2026–2030 strategy. This is a hands-on position and highly demanding role, in which will have the following key responsibilities: Implement a comprehensive fundraising strategy, actively seek out new funding opportunities, and cultivate relationships with private donors; Support the development and oversee the delivery of diverse partnerships and fundraising portfolio tailored to the needs of our network of organizations serving older people; Involve co-creating, building upon the current fundraising strategy, planning, executing, and monitoring partnerships and fundraising activities; Collaborate directly with the Secretary General and the Finance Manager and, as the partnerships/fundraising portfolio is extensive and varied, you will also work with the Policy and Communications teams. Your qualifications Essential At least five years of professional experience in fundraising development and tools such as pitches, proposals, due diligence, pipeline mapping etc., preferably within the non-profit sector; Commitment to the cause of human rights in general, and to age equality throughout life in particular; Minimum knowledge of EU social policies including human rights aspects; Have a pragmatic, creative and innovative approach to problem solving and decision making; Excellent time management and organisation skills, to be able to manage multiple tasks at once; Proficiency in English (writing and speaking) and excellent communication skills to communicate and build relationships with an array of people; Strong networking and relationship-building abilities; Enthusiastic and proactive, with perseverance and a can-do attitude; Enjoy collaborating with other team members. Desirable Previous experience in NGO sector and in the fields of social/ageing/human rights policies; Some experience in budget setting and management; Experienced in IT - using databases to log and keep information accurately up to date, in a timely manner; Some knowledge of French. How to apply for the post Please submit to maciej.kucharczyk@age-platform.eu : a copy of your CV/resume, and a cover letter (maximum 300 words) outlining your interest in age equality throughout life and in the role and how you fulfil the requirements set out in this job announcement. Only candidates shortlisted for interviews will be contacted. The deadline for application: Sunday 16 August, 23:59 CEST (Brussels time) Interviews (two rounds): Will take place online: first, in late September and, second, in October Starting date: Ideally 1 November 2026 AGE is an equal opportunity employer. We actively promote equality, diversity and inclusion. Don't forget to mention EuroBrussels when applying.
item.dc:creator: AGE Platform Europe

item.title: Online Safety Commissioner
item.description: Online Safety Commissioner Coimisiún na Meán Dublin, Ireland € 200,698 per year Job Summary: The role of Online Safety Commissioner is a statutory post, as set out in Section 11 of the Broadcasting Act 2009 (as amended by the Online Safety and Media Regulation Act, 2022). The Online Safety Commissioner plays a key role in the design, development and implementation of all Coimisiún na Meán’s strategies, policies and procedures. In particular, the Online Safety Commissioner has a key role in developing and implementing Ireland’s Online Safety Framework, in conjunction with the Digital Services Commissioner. The Online Safety Commissioner has primary responsibility for shaping policy direction, deepening understanding of online harms, and leading the evolution of the regulatory framework, including the integration of new legislative competences. They work closely with the Digital Services Commissioner who has primary responsibility for the supervision and enforcement of platform compliance and for representing Coimisiún na Meán within the EU institutional framework, including on the Board for Digital Services. Main Duties of the Job: Be part of the collective leadership of the organisation, supporting our growing teams, fostering our values and culture, ensuring collaboration across the organisation and taking shared responsibility for delivery across the Commission’s full mandate; Participate in decision-making on matters reserved to the Commission and on significant regulatory, policy and governance decisions across the entire Coimisiún na Meán mandate; In collaboration with the Digital Services Commissioner, oversee the work of Coimisiún na Meán related to the online safety framework ensuring alignment between policy, regulatory design and enforcement; Interact with relevant Government agencies and departments, in particular the Department of Culture, Communications and Sport and support relationships with external stakeholders through collaboration regarding online safety issues, including, for example, through the Digital Regulators Group, the Global Online Safety Regulators Network and/or representing An Coimisiún in Oireachtas settings and the media. A full list of duties and eligibility criteria can be found in the Candidate information Booklet . Information About the Organisation: Established in March 2023, Coimisiún na Meán is Ireland’s commission for regulating broadcasters and online media, and for supporting media development. Coimisiún na Meán’s vision is for a thriving, diverse, creative, safe and trusted media landscape. Its mission is to regulate, support and develop a media landscape that underpins fundamental rights and fosters an open, democratic and pluralistic society. Coimisiún na Meán’s strategic goals are to create a media landscape which serves the needs of children, underpins democracy, can be trusted by consumers, reflects and shapes Irish society, promotes diversity, equality and inclusion, and strengthens public health and safety. Benefits: See Information Booklet . The Selection Process: For more information and to apply , please use the Apply Now button below. The closing date for receipt of completed applications is 3pm on Thursday, 23 rd of July 2026 . If you feel you would benefit from a confidential discussion about this role, please contact Ms. Louise McEntee at louise.mcentee@publicjobs.ie . We are committed to a policy of equal opportunity and encourage applications under all nine grounds of the Employment Equality Act. Apply Now Deadline 23 July Don't forget to mention EuroBrussels when applying.

item.title: Funding Officer
item.description: Funding Officer CEB - Council of Europe Development Bank Paris, France € 91,829 - € 113,312 per year Grade: A2/A3 Salary: €91,829 to €113,312 tax-exempt per annum (equivalent to €7,652.42–€9,442.63 per month), plus allowances and benefits Contract: Four-year fixed-term contract with the possibility of renewal, and a potential conversion into an indefinite term. Closing date: 9 August 2026 Shape the funding that enables social progress across Europe. The Council of Europe Development Bank (CEB) is the only multilateral development bank with an exclusively social mandate. Working on behalf of its 43 member countries, we finance projects that improve lives through better education, healthcare, affordable housing and support for vulnerable communities, while addressing the social dimensions of climate change and the environment. Our Finance Directorate plays a vital role in ensuring the Bank has the financial strength and resilience to deliver that mission. Through sustainable funding strategies, investor engagement and prudent balance sheet management, we secure the resources that enable long-term investment in social development across Europe. As a Funding Officer within the Funding and Investor Relations Unit, you will help execute the Bank's annual funding programme across international capital markets. Working at the intersection of funding strategy, sustainable finance and investor relations, you will contribute to bond issuances, monitor global markets, support funding transactions and build relationships with institutional investors and market participants worldwide. This is an opportunity for an experienced capital markets professional to apply technical expertise within an international organisation where financial excellence directly supports social impact. What you'll be doing You will support the planning and execution of the CEB's annual funding programme, assisting with the structuring, pricing and documentation of bond issuances across international capital markets. You'll contribute to hedging operations, monitor market conditions and identify opportunities arising from developments in interest rates, investor demand and the broader macroeconomic environment. Alongside funding execution, you'll help strengthen relationships with investors through presentations, roadshows and market communications. You'll also contribute to the Bank's Social Inclusion Bond Framework, prepare briefing papers for senior management, analyse market intelligence and help develop funding and sustainable finance databases that support informed decision-making. You'll bring A Master's degree (or equivalent) in finance, economics, engineering or a related discipline; At least five years' experience in capital markets, including strong knowledge of fixed income markets and derivatives; Practical experience using Bloomberg and Refinitiv Workspace; Experience designing and managing databases; Advanced Excel skills, including complex formulae, together with strong IT and analytical capability; Excellent written and presentation skills; Strong problem-solving ability and sound judgement under pressure; Excellent command of either English or French and a good working knowledge of the other, and a willingness to develop it; Citizenship of one of the CEB's member states. If you're looking to combine technical excellence in international capital markets with meaningful social impact, we'd like to hear from you. How to apply Submit your CV and cover letter via the CEB’s online application system. Applications are welcome in English or French and must be submitted by midnight Paris time on 9 August 2026 . Only nationals of CEB Member States are eligible. Please indicate where you saw the vacancy advertised. Apply Now Deadline 9 August Don't forget to mention EuroBrussels when applying.
item.dc:creator: CEB - Council of Europe Development Bank

item.title: HR Officer
item.description: Your role Joining us as HR Officer (B5) you will be part of the HR Operations team and work closely with the HR Business Partners to support the Centre’s staff in Bonn. You serve as the first point of contact for routine staff inquiries regarding working conditions, contractual matters, and other employment-related issues. You will also be responsible for all aspects of HR administration, including the maintenance and organisation of staff records and data in our ERP system. As part of our HR team you collaborate closely with the organisation to address both day-to-day demands of the organisation and our staff. Our HR Operations team is central to ensuring that core HR processes are executed efficiently and reliably. About the HR Team The effectiveness of the organisation relies on strong HR professionals who work closely with line management to address both operational and strategic people matters. The central HR Operations team plays a key role in ensuring that core HR processes are delivered efficiently and consistently. The HR Officer in Bonn will be an integral member of the HR Operations team, working closely with colleagues in Reading and collaborating across locations to provide mutual support and ensure service continuity. Your responsibilities Act as first point of contact for routine staff questions on employment conditions, the ECMWF staff regulations and procedures, contractual matters, entitlements and benefits, escalating complex issues as appropriate; Draft and issue statutory correspondence and formal HR communications; Liaise with the relevant German authorities as appropriate; Manage the Protocol ID card application process for new staff and process change of circumstances for existing staff; Coordinate and process contractual changes requested by departments; Prepare employment offers, contracts of employment and related documents; Support the offer management process by liaising with successful candidates on all matters relating to their appointment prior to their commencement of employment; Coordinate the onboarding and induction process for newly recruited staff; Assist staff with applications relating to local registrations and administrative requirements; Perform ERP data entry, including payroll-related inputs, in close collaboration with the Payroll Manager, ensuring a high level of data accuracy and integrity; Monitor sick leave and absence records in line with organisational procedures; Contribute to HR projects and continuous improvement initiatives as required. About ECMWF The European Centre for Medium-Range Weather Forecasts (ECMWF) is a world-leader in weather and environmental forecasting. As an international organisation we serve our members and the wider community with global weather predictions and data that is critical for understanding and solving the climate crisis. We function as a 24/7 research and operational centre with a focus on medium and long-range predictions, holding one of the largest meteorological data archives in the world. The success of our activities builds on the talent of our scientists and experts, strong partnerships with 35 Member and Co-operating States and the international community, some of the most powerful supercomputers in the world, and the use of innovative technologies and machine learning across our operations. ECMWF has also developed a strong partnership with the European Union and has been entrusted with the implementation and operation of the Destination Earth Initiative and the Climate Change and Atmosphere Monitoring Services of the Copernicus Programme. Other areas of work include High Performance Computing and the development of digital tools that enable ECMWF to extend provision of data and products covering weather, climate, air quality, fire and flood prediction and monitoring. Our vision: The strength of a common goal. Our mission: Deliver global numerical weather predictions focusing on the medium-range and monitoring of the Earth system to and with our Member States. ECMWF is a multi-site organisation, with a main office in Reading, UK, a data centre/supercomputer in Bologna, Italy, and a large presence in Bonn, Germany. We appreciate the need for flexibility in the way our staff work and staff have the option to mix office work and teleworking (up to 10 days/month). See www.ecmwf.int for more info about what we do. About the Copernicus Programme Copernicus is the earth observation component of the European Union (EU) space programme. Based on the exploitation of spaced based and in situ (earth-based) observations and scientific models, Copernicus provides information services for land, marine, atmospheric and climate monitoring, as well as emergency management and security. These services, and their free, open and quality assured data and tools, support a range of environmental and security applications across sectors and policy domains. For details, see www.copernicus.eu . The Copernicus Atmosphere Monitoring Service (CAMS) service provides consistent and quality-controlled information related to air pollution and health, solar energy, greenhouse gases and climate forcing, everywhere in the world. For details, see https://atmosphere.copernicus.eu . The Copernicus Climate Change Service (C3S) service provides authoritative information about the past, present and future climate, as well as tools to enable climate change mitigation and adaptation strategies by policy makers and businesses. For details, see https://climate.copernicus.eu . What we're looking for Strong relationship management skills, with the ability to build trust and credibility quickly while maintaining strict confidentiality; Proven ability to manage multiple priorities and deliver to tight deadlines; High level of attention to detail and accuracy; Proactive approach, with the ability to work independently with limited supervision; Strong IT skills, with a willingness to fully utilise ERP systems; Collaborative team player, capable of leading on specific tasks while supporting wider team objectives; Solution-oriented and pragmatic mindset, with a focus on continuous process improvement. Education Candidates should have a higher standard of education (EQF Level 4). Experience, Knowledge, Skills Strong experience of working in an HR administrative capacity, ideally in an international environment; Experience of providing HR support to line managers and individuals; Excellent working knowledge of Microsoft Office (Word and Excel) is required; Strong written communication skills to draft and respond to correspondence and enquiries; Effective organisational and time management skills; Candidates must be able to work effectively in English; Fluency in German (spoken and written) is required given the required interactions with German authorities. We encourage you to apply even if you feel you don't precisely meet all these criteria. Other information Grade remuneration The successful candidate will be recruited according to the scales of the Co-ordinated Organisations. ECMWF offers a generous benefits package - full details of salary scales, allowances and conditions of employment are available on the ECMWF website at www.ecmwf.int/en/about/jobs . Starting date: 1 October 2026 Location: Bonn, Germany. Candidates are expected to relocate to the duty station. Interviews will take place via videoconference (MS Team). If you require any special accommodations in order to participate fully in our recruitment process, please contact us via email: jobs@ecmwf.int . Who can apply Applicants are invited to complete the online application form by clicking on the apply button below. At ECMWF, we consider an inclusive environment as key for our success. We are dedicated to ensuring a workplace that embraces diversity and provides equal opportunities for all, without distinction as to race, gender, age, marital status, social status, disability, sexual orientation, religion, personality, ethnicity and culture. We value the benefits derived from a diverse workforce and are committed to having staff that reflect the diversity of the countries that are part of our community, in an environment that nurtures equality and inclusion. Applications are invited from nationals from ECMWF Member States and Cooperating States, listed below, as well as from all EU Member States. In these exceptional times, we also welcome applications from Ukrainian nationals for this vacancy. Applications from nationals from other countries may be considered in exceptional cases. ECMWF Member States and Co-operating States are: Austria, Belgium, Bulgaria, Croatia, Czech Republic, Denmark, Estonia, Finland, France, Hungary, Germany, Georgia, Greece, Iceland, Ireland, Israel, Italy, Latvia, Lithuania, Luxembourg, Montenegro, Morocco, the Netherlands, Norway, North Macedonia, Portugal, Romania, Serbia, Slovakia, Slovenia, Spain, Sweden, Switzerland, Türkiye and the United Kingdom.
item.dc:creator: ECMWF - European Centre for Medium-Range Weather Forecasts

item.title: Communications Intern
item.description: Communications Intern POLIS Brussels, Belgium € 1,095 per month We are looking for a new Communications Intern to join our Brussels-based Secretariat. The Intern will help to enhance the visibility of POLIS and its members from across Europe. They will be part of the Communications team, managing POLIS social media profiles and the social media calendar; collecting POLIS social media analytics from LinkedIn, Bluesky, and Instagram; drafting articles for the POLIS website and Members’ Area; supporting the production and publication of digital and print publications, including POLIS' Cities in Motion magazine; producing short video clips and visual content for all of POLIS' digital channels; providing, when needed, communications support for POLIS projects and Working Groups under the guidance of the Communications and Membership Lead; taking notes and reporting on in-person and online events to support communications activities; and assisting with other communications tasks as required. The Intern will also support the preparation and editing of the Annual POLIS Conference communications materials, assist the Conference Team with printing, organising, and displaying signage at the venue, and handle ad hoc conference-related tasks as needed. Profile The successful candidate should have: a degree (studies completed), preferably in the field of communications/journalism or related to transport, urban planning, or similar; first-hand experience with communications, journalism, or public relations, including, to some degree: online content development (the ability to draft news and events for websites and social media posts for social media platforms); website content management, with a good understanding of WordPress as a plus; social media management, with experience on LinkedIn, Bluesky, and Instagram as a plus; visual content development, having an eye for graphic design and knowledge of Canva will be considered an asset; an interest in urban mobility; an excellent command of written and spoken English; knowledge of additional European languages will be an asset; the legal right to work in Belgium (POLIS cannot provide visa sponsorship; candidates must already hold the necessary residence or work authorisation to work in Belgium). Apply Now Deadline 24 July Don't forget to mention EuroBrussels when applying.

item.title: EU Projects Officer
item.description: EU Projects Officer European Cancer Organisation Brussels, Belgium Are you dynamic, organised, and motivated by impact? Are you interested in project management, public health, and EU funding programmes? Do you want to contribute to a mission that improves the lives of cancer patients, survivors, and their families across Europe? If so, the European Cancer Organisation (ECO) may be looking for you. In this full-time Brussels-based role, you will support a portfolio of EU-funded projects contributing to Europe’s Beating Cancer Plan and the EU Cancer Mission. You will work closely with experts, clinicians, patients, carers and policymakers to help deliver meaningful change in cancer prevention, care, and quality of life. Your Responsibilities As EU Projects Officer, you will contribute to the effective coordination and delivery of EU-funded projects in which ECO participates. You will be responsible for the monitoring of timelines and deliverables, ensuring smooth internal workflows, maintaining organised project records, and helping prepare materials such as agendas, presentations, reports, and meeting minutes. You will be a main point of contact for project partners, ensuring clear communication and the timely exchange of information. You will also play a role in the organisation of meetings and events, working closely with the ECO Operations team to manage scheduling, logistics, and follow-up. The role also includes supporting ECO’s participation in new project proposals, from monitoring new calls to drafting elements of project applications. You will help coordinate internal inputs and liaise with colleagues involved in drafting contributions. Finally, you will contribute to ECO’s broader engagement with EU-funded initiatives relevant to cancer policy, extending beyond the projects in which ECO is a direct partner. Who We’re Looking For We are seeking a motivated and proactive professional who is eager to grow within the EU health policy funding landscape. You should be highly organised, attentive to detail, and comfortable managing multiple parallel tasks. Strong communication and interpersonal skills are essential, as is the ability to present information clearly, to the point and maintain constructive relationships with diverse partners. You should enjoy problem-solving, be willing to take initiative, and be able to keep calm and focused when navigating complexity or dealing with unexpected challenges.1-2 years of prior professional experience in project management is required, with knowledge of EU funding programmes (particularly EU4Health and Horizon Europe) being an advantage. Fluency in written and spoken English is critical, and proficiency in additional European languages is welcome. Proficiency using Microsoft Office tools,particularly Excel, Word, PowerPoint, Outlook, and Teams, is required. We welcome applicants who demonstrate strong organisational ability, motivation to learn, and a commitment to high-quality work. Apply Now Deadline 16 August Don't forget to mention EuroBrussels when applying.

item.title: Senior Researcher ‘Just Transition/Foresight’ (m/f/x)
item.description: Senior Researcher ‘Just Transition/Foresight’ (m/f/x) ETUI - European Trade Union Institute Brussels, Belgium From € 6195 per month gross Status: Full-time – Permanent Department: Foresight Unit Application Deadline: 1 st September 2026 Estimated starting date: January 2027 ABOUT THE ETUI The ETUI provides research and training support to the European trade union movement. The Brussels-based Institute conducts research into European issues of strategic importance for the world of labour and promotes education and training activities with the aim of enhancing the capacity of workers’ organisations to contribute to social dialogue and building a social Europe. ABOUT THE POSITION The ETUI is seeking a senior researcher, ideally with team-leading experience, to join our staff. Working within a dynamic, international environment, you will be responsible for conducting research into future-oriented topics of relevance for the world of work, in particular Just Transition. KEY RESPONSIBILITIES Conducting own empirical research into future-oriented topics of relevance for the European trade unions and policymakers, in particular Just Transition and the economic, employment and social implications of structural transformations, be they technology or policy-driven; Communicating research findings to diverse audiences (academia, trade unions, policymakers, media); Contribute to the conception and coordination of the ETUI’s work programme in the area of foresight/just transition and monitor progress of the work programme in this area. QUALIFICATIONS AND SKILLS REQUIRED A PhD in a relevant social science discipline (economics, political economy, sociology, political science); Strong quantitative skills; Familiarity with foresight and scenario-building methodologies; Proven research excellence; Ability to communicate complex material to different audiences; Excellent interpersonal and communication skills, with the ability to work in a multilingual and multicultural environment. KNOWLEDGE AND EXPERIENCE At least 7 years’ relevant experience; experience in a non-profit or international organisation with a research focus is an asset; Proficiency in relevant econometric/data software and standard ICT tools (MS Office 365, including SharePoint and Teams); Project-management experience is essential and team-leading experience an asset; Excellent command of English; French and other languages are a significant asset; Knowledge of European Union institutions and of the trade union movement is a distinct advantage; Experience of working in a politically sensitive environment is an advantage. CONDITIONS OF EMPLOYMENT The ETUI offers a challenging role in a dynamic environment that bridges academic research and European policymaking. We provide a competitive salary and an attractive benefits package in line with qualifications and experience. The ETUI is an equal opportunities employer. Starting salary for Category A2 is €6195 gross per month (based on full-time employment). In accordance with the ETUI’s internal policy, this role allows for a maximum of 10 days of remote work per month (pro-rata for part-time staff). HOW TO APPLY Please address your application (CV, motivation letter, supporting documents) to Andrew Watt, General director at vacancy@etui.org by 1 September 2026 . We appreciate the interest of all applicants; however, please note that only shortlisted candidates will be formally contacted to proceed to the next stage (written assignment and panel interview in September 2026). We thank you for your understanding and for your interest in the ETUI. GDPR: The ETUI processes your personal data for recruitment purposes in accordance with our Internal Recruitment Policy. Your application files will be retained for a maximum of 3 months following the conclusion of the recruitment process, after which they will be permanently deleted. Don't forget to mention EuroBrussels when applying.
item.dc:creator: ETUI - European Trade Union Institute

item.title: Vice-chair Appeals Committee of the European Patent Oﬃce
item.description: Vice-chair Appeals Committee of the European Patent Oﬃce EPO - European Patent Office Munich, Germany The European Patent Office (EPO) is the second largest public institution in Europe. With headquarters in Munich, a branch at The Hague, sub-offices in Vienna and Berlin and a small bureau in Brussels, it employs approximately 6 200 staff across all four sites and has a self-financed annual budget of over EUR 2.5bn. The EPO is seeking to appoint vice‑chair for its Appeals Committee. The Appeals Committee is an advisory administrative body charged with reviewing disputed administrative decisions as part of the EPO's formal dispute settlement procedures. It is tasked with preparing reasoned opinions on internal appeals filed by staff - current or former - against adverse decisions or acts by the President of the EPO or the Administrative Council. The Appeals Committee usually sits in chambers composed of a chair or vice‑chair and staff members. It is supported in dealing with appeals by a secretariat composed of specialised lawyers and administrative staff. Specifics A vice-chair must possess the qualifications required for appointment to high judicial office or be a lawyer with experience in the area of employment law acquired at national or international level, who are not EPO employees in active employment and who have not been EPO employees within the past ten years. Job purpose A vice-chair is responsible for the overall conduct of the work of the Appeals Committee and the internal appeal proceedings as defined in the Service Regulations for permanent and other employees of the EPO (Service Regulations) and the Appeals Committee's own Rules of Procedure. A vice-chair is guided by the mission to provide a timely, fair and independent appeals process consistent with fair trial principles. There are six to ten Appeals Committee sessions per year split between chambers presided over by either the chair or a vice‑chair. Key tasks and responsibilities/accountabilities Managing all Appeals Committee operations and participating in and guiding all written and oral procedural stages of proceedings before the Appeals Committee, up to and including the adoption of reasoned opinions; Serving as presiding member of Appeals Committee chambers formed in accordance with the relevant legal framework, involving responsibility for procedural decisions, notably on the holding of hearings, the initiation of test-case procedures and the consolidation of appeals; As presiding member of a chamber, the vice-chair will encourage and actively facilitate amicable settlements between the parties to proceedings. Other elements A vice-chair is assisted by a secretariat composed of: a director; a head of department; lawyers; administrative staff. Minimum qualifications Diploma of completed university studies at master's level or - in exceptional cases - equivalent professional experience; Qualifications required for appointment to high judicial office or being a lawyer with experience in the area of employment law acquired at national or international level; Proven very high standards of performance; Impeccable integrity and highest moral standards and authority; Experience in employment law mediation or amicable settlement proceedings in the workplace would be an asset; Procedures before the Appeals Committee can be conducted in any of the EPO's official languages (English, French and German). Suitable candidates are therefore expected to have a very good command of two of the official languages, and the ability to understand the third language would be an advantage. Other elements The Service Regulations are available online at: www.epo.org/about-us/office/service-regulations.html ; The composition of the Appeals Committee is laid down in Article 111 of the Service Regulations; The functioning of the Appeals Committee is laid down in Article 111a of the Service Regulations; The Rules of Procedure of the Appeals Committee are part of the implementing rules and additional texts included with the Service Regulations. Remuneration Vice-chairs are paid a flat rate per opinion (or amicable settlement). The flat rate covers preparatory work, hearings (if any) and follow-up work. Travel costs and daily subsistence allowances are paid separately upon invoice. Process and timeline The successful candidate will be appointed for a period of three years, and may be reappointed. Selection will be made on the basis of qualifications and interview(s). The closing date for applications is 7 September 2026 . It is intended to hold the interviews in calendar weeks 38-39 of 2026. Key information Duration of contract: 3 years Application deadline: 7 September 2026 Interviews planned for: calendar weeks 38-39 We welcome all applications for vacant positions. We consider each on its professional merits - regardless of your nationality, ethnic origin, gender, sexual orientation, marital status, disability, parental responsibilities, age, religion or belief. Apply Now Deadline 7 September Don't forget to mention EuroBrussels when applying.
item.dc:creator: EPO - European Patent Office

item.title: Expression of Interest (EOI) - Roster of Individual Consultants for International IDEA's Ukraine Democracy-Assistance Portfolio
item.description: Expression of Interest (EOI) - Roster of Individual Consultants for International IDEA's Ukraine Democracy-Assistance Portfolio International IDEA - The International Institute for Democracy and Electoral Assistance Reference No: 2026-06-094 Deadline for Submissions: Proposals must be submitted on or before 23:59 (CET), 21 August, 2026 . Late submissions will not be considered for evaluation. Address for Submissions: E-mail: ukraine.roster@idea.int This email address is set up with auto-response to acknowledge emails received. In the event that you did not receive an autoreply, please send a text message prior to the deadline for submission (NO CALLS, CALLS WILL NOT BE ANSWERED) to this number +46725375735 prior to the deadline for submission . In your text message, please provide the following: EOI reference no. and title; Email address you used for submission; Date and Time, you sent your submission (e.g. 01 December 2021, 12:30 CET ); The above information must also be sent to eoi@idea.int before the deadline for submission. Format for Submissions: Applicants are requested to submit their application by email, following the submission requirements set out in Section 2 of the EOI. Email subject line: Use the format "EOI 2026-06-094 – [Your Full Name]" . Example: EOI 2026-06-094 – John Smith Email Address for Clarifications: eoi@idea.int Clarifications may be requested via e-mail no later than 7 August prior to the submission deadline at the above email address. Note a response to a request for clarifications will be issued on our website http://www.idea.int/ . Therefore, please check the website regularly during the process. Important Note: there are two different email addresses as outlined above, one to be used for Submission of your final proposal and the second one to be used for clarifications and other related correspondence. Section 1. Background About International IDEA The International Institute for Democracy and Electoral Assistance (International IDEA) is an intergovernmental organization with a mission to advance democracy worldwide as a universal human aspiration and an enabler of sustainable development. We do this by shaping, strengthening, and safeguarding democratic political institutions and processes at all levels. About the Ukraine Democracy-Assistance Portfolio International IDEA implements a democracy-assistance portfolio in Ukraine focused on supporting the country's democratic institutions and electoral processes in a particularly demanding context. The portfolio covers a broad range of workstreams, including electoral assistance, electoral risk management, strategic communications, gender mainstreaming, political finance, parliamentary strengthening, constitutional building, EU integration, political party assistance, countering disinformation, FIMI, ICT and cybersecurity for electoral management bodies, social media monitoring, and capacity building through the Central Election Commission's Training Centre. To support the effective implementation of this portfolio, International IDEA seeks to establish a roster of qualified individual consultants – both international and national - with subject matter expertise across these workstreams. We now seek expressions of interest from qualified individuals to add to a roster of consultants from which we may call off services as required. Inclusion in the roster is not a guarantee that you will be contracted for any work. Each piece of work will have terms of reference drafted which will include expected deliverables and an anticipated timeframe for delivery. If your skills match the requirements, you will then be requested to confirm your availability and provide a quotation for the work required. Several consultants may be approached to provide quotations for each piece of work. If agreement is reached, a contract will be entered into for that work. Inclusion in the roster does not constitute a guarantee of any contract with International IDEA. When a consultant is selected for a specific assignment, a Supply Contract will be issued in line with International IDEA's procurement policies and procedures. Please note that if you respond to this EOI we will include your CV in a roster that may be accessed by Staff Members for the purposes of determining your suitability to be approached for specific assignments. Please see our Privacy Policy ( Privacy Policy | International IDEA ) to understand how we treat your personal data. Consultants accepted into the roster will be retained for a period of five (5) years from the date of the EOI award. International IDEA reserves the right to extend the validity period of the roster as needed. If your contact details or other relevant information change during the validity of the roster, or if you wish to withdraw your application or be removed from the roster at any time, please notify International IDEA by email at consultants.roster@idea.int . Section 2. Requirements Applicants interested in being included in the roster are requested to submit the documents listed below as part of their application. Please ensure that the documents are arranged in the order listed below. 2.1 A cover letter with your motivation for applying to be part of the roster. 2.2 A CV in English which clearly outlines how your details match the requirements and including: Your experience with relevant dates; Your educational qualifications; and The names and contact details of at least two references. 2.3 A completed Roster Questionnaire (Annex A). The Roster Questionnaire includes information on your thematic areas of expertise, regional experience, years of experience, language proficiency (English, Ukrainian, and other languages), proposed daily rate in EUR, and bank information for future payment purposes. 2.4 A Declaration of Honour form signed. A completed and signed declaration of honour form. For Section VI – Declaration on honour on restrictive measures: Please tick “YES” to confirm that the candidate/tenderer is not subject to any restrictive measures or sanctions as described in this section. Section 3. Criteria 3.1 Applications will be reviewed based on the documentation submitted. Inclusion in the roster will be based on the following criteria: Relevant professional experience in the thematic area(s) selected; Academic qualifications and professional certifications; Regional expertise, particularly in Ukraine or similar contexts; Language proficiency in English and Ukrainian; Proposed daily rate in EUR, considered in relation to the level of expertise and in line with International IDEA's policies for individual consultants. 3.2 Applicants may apply to multiple thematic areas in a single submission. Section 4. Final Considerations 4.1 International IDEA will not be bound to select any of the consultants or firms that apply and reserves the right to cancel this EOI at any stage of the process without any obligation to applicants. 4.2 The following documents are enclosed with this EOI Cover Page: Annex A – Roster Questionnaire; Annex B – Declaration of Honour Form; General Terms and Conditions. 4.3 Complaints regarding any aspect of the EOI process should be addressed in writing to both the Internal Auditor and the Executive Director at International IDEA. The address is tender.complaints@idea.int . The Expression of Interest Notice, Roster Questionnaire, Declaration of Honour Form and General Terms and Conditions are available at: https://www.idea.int/about-us/procurement-and-tenders . Don't forget to mention EuroBrussels when applying.
item.dc:creator: International IDEA - The International Institute for Democracy and Electoral Assistance
